channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Sun, 07 Jun 2026 19:47:31 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: 'No sanctions relief, asset unfreezing or Lebanon talks': Donald Trump lays down terms for Iran deal
item.description: US President Donald Trump stated that sanctions relief and asset unfreezing for Iran are contingent on a peace deal, with potential future concessions if Tehran "behaves." He clarified Lebanon's exclusion from short-term agreements. Trump emphasized Iran's need to face consequences after decades of perceived impunity, noting the conflict's 100-day mark without a permanent resolution.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Deaf neighbour recounts tragic moments after Gurshabad Singh was found in Auckland pool
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Couldn't finish it all': Trump's border czar tries food at ICE detention facility after complaints
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Dubai-based Indian man, wife killed in Kerala road crash; daughter survives after car switch
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Saudi Arabia arrests more than 7,700 residency, labour and border violators in one-week crackdown
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK deputy PM tells Vance he is ‘wrong’ on Henry Nowak murder by Sikh man Vickrum Digwa
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Chernobyl fuel facility hit: Nuclear fears resurface, Kyiv says Russian attack ‘deliberate'
item.description: A Russian drone struck a spent nuclear fuel storage facility in Ukraine's Chornobyl exclusion zone, officials reported. While authorities confirmed radiation levels remained normal, President Zelenskyy condemned the attack as a deliberate act of recklessness targeting critical infrastructure. This incident adds to a pattern of Russian actions endangering nuclear safety, with Kyiv accusing Moscow of systemic nuclear blackmail.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 100 days on, Ali Khamenei remains unburied: What's delaying the funeral of Iran's longest-serving supreme leader?
item.description: Over 100 days after his death in a US-Israel strike, Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has not been buried. The prolonged delay, attributed to security concerns for his successor Mojtaba Khamenei and potential damage to the remains, has fueled speculation and left the nation in a state of confusion regarding the funeral and leadership transition.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Triple talaq' on WhatsApp: Rajasthan woman files case against husband in Kuwait
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Several A-list celebrities reportedly reject invitations to attend Dana White's Trump-linked UFC event
item.description: Days before UFC Freedom 250 transforms the White House South Lawn into an outdoor fight arena, reports suggest several celebrities invited by Dana White, including Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson, Adam Sandler and Jared Leto, will not attend. The unusual event, tied to America's 250th anniversary celebrations, continues to generate attention for its political associations, unique setting and high-profile guest list.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: 'The diamond was gorgeous': Jill Biden wanted to keep PM Modi's $20,000 gift but couldn't afford it
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Gunshots, screams & panic - Ohio mass shooting caught on cam, several injured
item.description: Panic erupted near Toledo's Old West End Festival on Saturday as gunfire injured at least 12 people, with two in critical condition. Authorities suspect multiple shooters, possibly exchanging fire. The incident, which saw festival-goers fleeing for safety, is under ongoing investigation. Governor DeWine emphasized the need for safe community events.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Henry Nowak's killer Vickrum Digwa 'threatened' Sikh worshipper at Southampton Gurdwara months before fatal attack: Report
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Think and live in the real world': Iran reacts to Trump's 'ready for meeting' remark
item.description: In a recent statement, Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi firmly rejected US President Donald Trump's notion of a potential meeting with Iran's Supreme Leader, deeming it impractical amidst escalating tensions. Trump, who has yet to engage with the leader in person, had expressed openness to such a dialogue.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Albania’s luxury coastal plan linked to Trump sparks storm - why are people protesting?
item.description: Thousands of Albanians are protesting a major coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner, fearing the handover of untouched coastline to powerful investors. The scheme, planned for a wildlife reserve and a former military island, has drawn criticism from environmental groups and an anti-corruption investigation. Prime Minister Edi Rama defends the project as crucial for tourism and EU aspirations.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Three British men admit roles in death of Canadian restaurateur killed over $150 unpaid bill
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US Air Force scrambles to rebuild MQ-9 reaper fleet after heavy combat losses
item.description: As the US Air Force navigates significant MQ-9 Reaper drone losses during its operations in the Middle East, they're turning to General Atomics for surplus aircraft. With their drone inventory shrinking, the military is also salvaging retired Predator drones for essential components. This strategy underscores the hurdles in keeping their aerial surveillance and strike capabilities at peak performance.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Han Seong-sook? South Korea first likely woman Prime Minister in 20 years
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Saved by the barrel: Why crude hasn't hit the $200 mark
item.description: Despite fears of $200 oil following Strait of Hormuz disruptions, prices remain below $100. This resilience is attributed to increased US exports, weaker Chinese demand, and alternative supply routes. While some shipping through the strait continues, overall transits are down. Emergency measures and subdued demand have averted the worst-case scenario, but market stability hinges on temporary solutions.
item.dc:creator: TOI Business Desk

item.title: Israeli soldiers open fire at car in West Bank; 7-month-old infant dies
item.description: In a heartbreaking incident in Hebron, a Palestinian infant lost his life after Israeli soldiers opened fire on his family's vehicle. The father recounted how they had paused and raised their hands in surrender moments before the shots erupted. According to the IDF, the troops perceived the car as a potential threat due to its speed.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
